name: explain-topic-workflow
description: A workflow that explains a topic using an analogy and gathers user questions.

inputs.topic: The topic the user wants explained

choose-analogy.do: Given the topic '{{topic}}', think of a vivid, everyday analogy that makes the core concepts intuitive. Output the analogy name and a one-sentence summary of why it fits.
choose-analogy.produces: An analogy name and a brief justification for why it maps well to the topic

explain-with-analogy.do: Using the chosen analogy, write a clear, engaging explanation of '{{topic}}'. Map each key concept of the topic to a concrete element in the analogy. Keep it conversational and accessible to someone with no prior knowledge. End with a short recap of the key takeaways.
explain-with-analogy.produces: A full explanation of the topic through the lens of the analogy, with key concept mappings and a recap

gather-questions.do: Present the explanation to the user, then use the AskUserQuestion tool to ask: 'What questions do you have about this topic? (Type "none" if everything is clear.)'
gather-questions.produces: The user's follow-up questions or confirmation that they have none

answer-questions.do: If the user provided follow-up questions, answer each one clearly, continuing to reference the analogy where helpful. If the user said 'none' or indicated no questions, skip ahead.
answer-questions.produces: Answers to the user's questions, or acknowledgement that no questions were asked
answer-questions.condition: The user asked one or more follow-up questions

summary.do: Write a brief summary that recaps: (1) the topic, (2) the analogy used, (3) the key concept mappings, and (4) any follow-up questions that were answered. Format it as a clean, readable reference the user can keep.
summary.produces: A concise reference summary of the entire explanation session
